timely_topic.name: AI Leadership: The Identity Gap
timely_topic.description: Concentrated 10-week campaign positioning SightShift as the authority on AI + leadership identity. Research articles, white paper, YouTube series, podcast episodes, character videos, Twitter/X launch.

items.title: Columbus Announcement: Why AI Outperforms Your Team
items.detail: Full newsletter list announcement. Leading with 'why does AI outperform your team' hook. New SightShift event. Include 'not in Columbus? tell us your city' ask.

items.title: Columbus Event Announcement
items.detail: Announcement post — new SightShift event format. 'Why AI Outperforms Your Team.' May 6, Columbus. Limited seats.

items.title: Columbus: Engagement/provocation post
items.detail: AI + leadership provocation that naturally ties to the Columbus event theme.

items.title: Columbus Segment Email #1
items.detail: Personal, direct email to past Columbus attendees and Ohio-area contacts. More intimate tone. 'We're doing something new and I want you in the room.'

items.title: STORY: "I used to think the problem was skills..."
items.detail: Chris's personal discovery of the identity gap. No data. No thesis. Just the moment he realized something deeper was happening. P.S. Columbus event mention.

items.title: Columbus Segment Email #2
items.detail: Follow-up to Ohio segment. 'Seats are filling. Here's what we're covering.' More detail on the event content.

items.title: Data graphic: "We studied 988 leaders. 69% hide under pressure."
items.detail: Data visualization post — headline stat from research. Tease Thursday's full article.

items.title: Data bomb: "988 leaders. 69% hide under pressure. AI doesn't care."
items.detail: Punchy data bomb tweet. Short, sharp, designed to stop the scroll.

items.title: FRAMEWORK: The two default modes — proving and hiding
items.detail: Teaching email: The two default modes every leader falls into under pressure — proving and hiding. Why AI amplifies both. P.S. Columbus event mention.

items.title: FRAMEWORK: The retreat that cost $40K and changed nothing
items.detail: They spent $40K on a leadership retreat. Trust falls, personality tests, a motivational speaker. Six months later, the VP resigned. You've seen this movie. The retreat wasn't wrong — it just wasn't deep enough.

items.detail: Revenue up. Team growing. Metrics green. But something was off and he couldn't name it. That's drift — and 97% of leaders can't see it from the inside.

items.title: STORY: The VP who stopped volunteering ideas
items.detail: She used to be the first to speak in meetings. Then AI tools showed up and she went quiet. Fear of being replaceable killed her imagination before AI ever could.

items.title: Ginosko: Why AI can generate but can't imagine
items.detail: Framework post introducing the first of three capacities AI can't replace.

items.title: Contrarian take: AI can't imagine
items.detail: AI generates based on patterns. Imagination requires seeing what isn't there yet.

items.title: ACTION: Ginosko — something essential is disappearing and you can feel it
items.detail: The capacity to imagine what doesn't exist. AI can generate, but it cannot imagine. Your team's imagination is the canary in the coal mine.

items.detail: AI-generated vision is not vision. It's pattern matching dressed up as strategy. This is the gap the IFQ reveals — are you leading or optimizing?

items.detail: When your AI agrees with you, that's not confirmation. It's a mirror with no reflection. You can automate analysis but you cannot automate judgment.
